name: MLC-LLM
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); no API costs; GPU/CPU hardware is cost

auth.notes: Local library; REST server mode has optional API key; no external service auth

performance.notes: Highly optimized via TVM compilation; often fastest local inference for specific hardware; mobile-capable

agent_readiness.notes: LLM inference via Apache TVM machine learning compilation. Compiles models to optimized native code per hardware (CUDA, Metal, OpenCL, Vulkan, WebGPU). Enables LLM inference on mobile devices, browsers (WebLLM), and edge hardware. Python/REST API. OpenAI-compatible server mode. Impressive on-device performance but complex setup.

gotchas: Complex setup — model compilation via TVM requires significant expertise
Model compilation can take hours for first-time setup
Best for edge/mobile deployment — may be overkill for standard server inference
best_when: Edge/mobile LLM deployment requiring maximum inference efficiency
Research on LLM inference optimization and hardware-specific compilation
IoT or specialized hardware targets needing custom compilation
avoid_when: Standard server inference (Ollama/vLLM simpler and well-supported)
Teams without ML systems expertise for TVM compilation
summary: MLC-LLM uses Apache TVM to compile LLMs into optimized native code for specific hardware (CUDA, Metal, OpenCL, Vulkan, WebGPU). Enables LLM inference on mobile devices and browsers (WebLLM) with impressive performance. Python/REST API with OpenAI-compatible server mode. Complex setup requiring TVM compilation. Best for edge/mobile deployment or browser-based inference (WebGPU). For standard servers, Ollama or vLLM is simpler.
